_meta.note: Phase-7 forensic traces — each produced by running the operator in a real WorldState; StateDeltas + follow-up events are the real objects.

traces.question: Will customer cust_042 (tenure=2mo, month-to-month, $79/mo) churn within the window?
traces.why_selected: won validation Brier; beat logistic on held-out (CI<0)
traces.posterior_context: features observed (customer record); tenure smooth carries the fitted declining-hazard shape; no Phase-3 latent needed for this observed-feature case

nonlinear_calculation.note: GAM raises risk for very-short tenure beyond the logistic's constant-slope prediction (declining-hazard curvature)

traces.terminal_effect: quantities[churn] set to True; downstream retention actions keyed on this churn outcome differ vs the logistic arm

anti_scaffolding.1_mechanism: attrition/dropout hazard — a subscription relationship ending
anti_scaffolding.2_why_nonlinear: tenure→churn hazard is strongly declining/convex (0.49→0.02 across tenure)
anti_scaffolding.3_candidates: logistic vs GAM(tenure,charges smooths)+interaction vs logistic+interaction
anti_scaffolding.4_baseline: additive logistic (the Phase-6 form) + constant base rate
anti_scaffolding.6_split: held-out; group-disjoint variant for the backtest
anti_scaffolding.7_selection: validation Brier, then parsimony vs logistic on TEST
anti_scaffolding.8_uncertainty: transport widening on log-odds; posterior path available when a latent enters
anti_scaffolding.10_history: n/a for the cross-sectional case; tenure IS the accumulated-time state
anti_scaffolding.11_posterior: none required here (observed features); Jensen-gap path validated separately
anti_scaffolding.13_transport: does NOT transport across contract types → domain_restricted (measured)
anti_scaffolding.14_scenario_object: nonlinear_spec bound to cust_042 features + fitted GAM params
anti_scaffolding.17_calculation: GAM σ(Σ linear + Σ smooth(tenure,charges) + interaction) = 0.6498
anti_scaffolding.19_future_event: none for a terminal churn resolution (recurrent path available)
anti_scaffolding.20_terminal_change: churn outcome + actor.outcome[churn] set; retention branch differs
anti_scaffolding.22_ablation: gam_no_interaction and single-smooth arms isolate the smooths' contribution
anti_scaffolding.23_failed: nonlinear transport across contract types failed (preserved)
anti_scaffolding.24_not_curve_fit: executes through WorldState→StateDelta→terminal churn quantity that downstream mechanisms read; not a standalone predictor

traces.question: How does a name's adoption share evolve from 2% given saturation at L=8%?
traces.why_selected: beats non-saturating extrapolation on real baby-name trajectories (paired CI<0)
traces.nonlinear_calculation: ΔS = r·S·(1−S/L): growth slows as S→L; the step count = 6 StateDeltas emitted
traces.future_events: each step schedules the next year's state_step (endogenous) until horizon
traces.terminal_effect: terminal share = 0.07581; a linear form would keep climbing past L (overshoot) — the saturation changes the terminal
traces.anti_scaffolding_summary: nonlinear saturation executed event-by-event through StateDelta; terminal share bounded by L, unlike the linear extrapolation

traces.question: With an uncertain Phase-3 half-saturation k, what is E[Hill response]?
traces.posterior_context: Phase-3 posterior over k (mean 4, sd 2); propagated per-particle

nonlinear_calculation.note: collapsing k to its mean would MIS-state the response by the Jensen gap — exactly what Part 12 forbids

traces.terminal_effect: the operator wrote the outcome using the posterior-correct E[f(X)], not the biased f(E[X])
